relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Truth and reverence shape Paul’s service.
Hope rests on the Divine promise.
The word appears in its season through proclamation.
Titus is a genuine child in common trust.
Titus remains in Crete to set lacking things in order.
Elders are measured by trustworthiness close to home.
The overseer is a steward, not self-willed or greedy.
Hospitality, justice, holiness, and self-control mark the steward.
Holding trustworthy teaching allows encouragement and correction.
Teaching for shameful gain must be stopped.
A sharp local saying is quoted and must not reduce a whole people.
Sharp correction aims at healthy trust.
Human commands turning from truth are refused.
Purity and defilement begin in mind and conscience.
Claimed knowledge of Divine is tested by works.

contemplative_note: Titus 1 is order without domination: steward-care, healthy teaching, household protection, and local polemic guarded against stereotyping.
